artifact.content_preview:
De : pierre.gagnon@ssc-spc.gc.ca
À : alice.tran@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Cc : marie.lefebvre@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Objet : Demande d'ajout — Modernisation des postes de travail des utilisateurs
Date : 8 mars 2026

Bonjour Alice,

Je vous écris pour soumettre une demande d'ajout de portée au projet
NGIS Phase 3 de la part du gestionnaire de prestation de services.

Dans le cadre de la consolidation des sites B et C, plusieurs employés
des centres de données affectés devront être relocalisés au site A à
Gatineau. Le

extracted_tasks.text: Remplacement de 85 ordinateurs portables (modèles Dell Latitude ou équivalent)
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: Remplacement de 85 ordinateurs portables... livraison et configuration avant le 15 avril 2026

extracted_tasks.text: Remplacement de 40 postes de travail fixes dans les salles de serveurs consolidées
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: Remplacement de 40 postes de travail fixes... livraison et configuration avant le 15 avril 2026

extracted_tasks.text: Examiner la demande d'ajout de portée et la soumettre au comité directeur
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: Pourriez-vous examiner cette demande et la soumettre au comité directeur lors de la prochaine réunion du 10 mars ?

extracted_tasks.text: Décision : Proposition d'inclure le remplacement des équipements dans le projet NGIS Phase 3
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: Le gestionnaire de prestation propose que le projet NGIS Phase 3 inclue également le remplacement de leurs équipements de travail

alerts.detected_ask: Replacement of 85 laptops and 40 desktop workstations for employees relocating from Sites B and C to Site A, estimated at $210,000–$240,000 CAD, to be delivered and configured by April 15, 2026.
alerts.baseline_reference: OUT OF SCOPE: 'End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile devices) — addressed under NGIS Phase 4'
alerts.suggested_action: Formally reject the scope addition request as submitted and issue a written scope clarification notice to Pierre Gagnon and Marie Lefebvre. Document the request in the project change log as a rejected change request (CR). Redirect the service delivery manager to NGIS Phase 4, which is the designated

```json
{
  "in_scope": [
    "Decommissioning of Sites B and C physical infrastructure",
    "Migration of all server workloads from Sites B and C to Site A",
    "Physical server rack consolidation and cable management at Site A",
    "Environmental systems (HVAC, UPS, fire suppression) optimization at Site A",
    "Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites",
    "Network segmentation reconfiguration per updated security zones",
    "BGP routing table migration and validation",
    "Network performance baseline testing post-migration",
    "Migration of all on-premise Active Directory domain controllers to consolidated infrastructure at Site A",
    "AD schema extension and forest restructuring",
    "Group Policy Object (GPO) consolidation and testing",
    "Integration testing with downstream applications dependent on AD",
    "Weekly status reports to SSC Project Management Office",
    "Steering committee presentations (monthly)",
    "Risk register maintenance",
    "Change management and stakeholder communications"
  ],
  "out_of_scope": [
    "End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile devices) — addressed under NGIS Phase 4",
    "Application modernization or re-platforming — no changes to application code, architecture, or hosting platform; lift and shift only",
    "GC-Cloud workload migration to public cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GC-Cloud) — addressed under GC-Cloud Wave 4",
    "End-user training and change management beyond project communications — responsibility of client departments",
    "Procurement of new application licenses — software licensing changes resulting from platform migration",
    "Network monitoring tool upgrades — NOC monitoring platform upgrades deferred to a separate initiative"
  ],
  "deliverables": [
    "D-001: Project Management Plan — Due October 31, 2024",
    "D-002: Technical Design Document (TDD) — Due December 15, 2024",
    "D-003: Security Assessment and Authorization (SA&A) Package — Due January 31, 2025",
    "D-004: Migration Runbook (Sites B and C) — Due February 28, 2025",
    "D-005: User Acceptance Test (UAT) Plan — Due March 10, 2026",
    "D-006: Site A Consolidation Complete (Go-Live) — Due April 30, 2026",
    "D-007: Post-Migration Validation Report — Due May 31, 2026",
    "D-008: Project Closure Report — Due June 30, 2026"
  ],
  "constraints": [
    "Total approved budget is $2,400,000 CAD inclusive of all fees, disbursements, and applicable taxes",
    "Expenditures beyond the approved budget require a formal budget amendment approved by the Director General of IT Infrastructure",
    "Budget variances exceeding 15% require submission to Treasury Board",
    "Go-Live (D-006) must be achieved no later than April 30, 2026",
    "Final project closure date is June 30, 2026 (end of fiscal year 2025–2026)",
    "Extensions beyond June 30, 2026 require ADM-level approval",
    "All work must comply with ITSG-33: IT Security Risk Management — A Lifecycle Approach",
    "All work must comply with the Government of Canada Cloud Adoption Strategy",
    "All work must comply with the Treasury Board Policy on Government Security",
    "All work must comply with the Privacy Act (R.S.C., 1985, c. P-21)",
    "All client-facing deliverables must comply with the Official Languages Act",
    "Scope, schedule, or budget changes must follow the SSC Project Change Management Process",
    "Scope changes require written approval from the Project Sponsor and must be assessed for cost and schedule impact before implementation"
  ]
}
```

llm_calls.content:
```json
{
  "alignment_score": 0.05,
  "drift_type": "contradicts_scope",
  "detected_ask": "Replacement of 85 laptops and 40 desktop workstations for employees relocating from Sites B and C to Site A, estimated at $210,000–$240,000 CAD, to be delivered and configured by April 15, 2026.",
  "baseline_reference": "OUT OF SCOPE: 'End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile devices) — addressed under NGIS Phase 4'",
  "suggested_action": "Formally reject the scope addition request as submitted and issue a written scope clarification notice to Pierre Gagnon and Marie Lefebvre. Document the request in the project change log as a rejected change request (CR). Redirect the service delivery manager to NGIS Phase 4, which is the designated vehicle for end-user device refresh. If the operational urgency is genuine, advise the requestor to initiate a formal scope change request under NGIS Phase 4 with their respective departmental authority. Do not table this request at the steering committee as a candidate for approval within Phase 3 without first completing a formal impact assessment — noting that the $210,000–$240,000 estimated cost would consume 8.75%–10% of the total approved $2,400,000 CAD budget, potentially triggering the 15% variance threshold when combined with other expenditures, and that the April 15, 2026 delivery deadline falls only 15 days before the April 30, 2026 Go-Live milestone (D-006), introducing unacceptable schedule risk.",
  "reasoning": "The request explicitly asks for end-user device replacement (laptops and desktop workstations), a category that is unambiguously listed as out of scope in the project baseline and explicitly deferred to NGIS Phase 4. There is no interpretive ambiguity here — the baseline language directly names 'laptops, desktops, mobile devices' as excluded. The operational rationale offered (co-location disruption, aging hardware, avoidance of a separate project) does not constitute a valid scope justification under the baseline governance framework; it is a convenience argument that does not override the formally approved scope boundary. Additionally, the request introduces compounding risks: (1) a budget impact of up to $240,000 CAD against an already fixed $2,400,000 CAD ceiling with no identified offset; (2) a compressed delivery window of approximately five weeks before Go-Live (D-006), with no buffer for procurement, imaging, and configuration; and (3) no mention of a formal change request, cost-benefit analysis, or Director General approval as required by the budget constraint clause. The alignment score of 0.05 reflects that the only marginal connection to the project is the physical relocation context — the substance of the ask is entirely outside the approved scope."
}
```

parsed_json.detected_ask: Replacement of 85 laptops and 40 desktop workstations for employees relocating from Sites B and C to Site A, estimated at $210,000–$240,000 CAD, to be delivered and configured by April 15, 2026.
parsed_json.baseline_reference: OUT OF SCOPE: 'End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile devices) — addressed under NGIS Phase 4'
parsed_json.suggested_action: Formally reject the scope addition request as submitted and issue a written scope clarification notice to Pierre Gagnon and Marie Lefebvre. Document the request in the project change log as a rejected change request (CR). Redirect the service delivery manager to NGIS Phase 4, which is the designated vehicle for end-user device refresh. If the operational urgency is genuine, advise the requestor to initiate a formal scope change request under NGIS Phase 4 with their respective departmental authority. Do not table this request at the steering committee as a candidate for approval within Phase 3 without first completing a formal impact assessment — noting that the $210,000–$240,000 estimated cost would consume 8.75%–10% of the total approved $2,400,000 CAD budget, potentially triggering the 15% variance threshold when combined with other expenditures, and that the April 15, 2026 delivery deadline falls only 15 days before the April 30, 2026 Go-Live milestone (D-006), introducing unacceptable schedule risk.
parsed_json.reasoning: The request explicitly asks for end-user device replacement (laptops and desktop workstations), a category that is unambiguously listed as out of scope in the project baseline and explicitly deferred to NGIS Phase 4. There is no interpretive ambiguity here — the baseline language directly names 'laptops, desktops, mobile devices' as excluded. The operational rationale offered (co-location disruption, aging hardware, avoidance of a separate project) does not constitute a valid scope justification under the baseline governance framework; it is a convenience argument that does not override the formally approved scope boundary. Additionally, the request introduces compounding risks: (1) a budget impact of up to $240,000 CAD against an already fixed $2,400,000 CAD ceiling with no identified offset; (2) a compressed delivery window of approximately five weeks before Go-Live (D-006), with no buffer for procurement, imaging, and configuration; and (3) no mention of a formal change request, cost-benefit analysis, or Director General approval as required by the budget constraint clause. The alignment score of 0.05 reflects that the only marginal connection to the project is the physical relocation context — the substance of the ask is entirely outside the approved scope.
